name: Rice Tabbouleh
subtitle: Tabboulet ruz
introduction:
This is a great way to use up leftover cooked rice, if you have any (but also delicious enough to start from scratch if you don’t!). If you do this you’ll need to start with 1 cup/190g of cooked rice. Using leftover rice is fine, as long as you chill what you are not going to use soon after it’s made, rather than letting it sit around at room temperature for too long. Take it out of the fridge about 20 minutes before you want to eat the tabbouleh, though; you don’t want it to be fridge-cold. You won’t need the olive oil, turmeric, or water here; just add the rice as it is with all the remaining ingredients.

Playing around: The rice can just as well be replaced by other grains, if you want. Quinoa is a gluten-free option, like the rice, and couscous and fregola also work well.

Getting ahead: Do all the chopping ahead of time, if you like—up to about 4 hours—and the rice can be made a full day ahead. It can be mixed in an hour or so before serving, but not much more than this.

ingredients.item: 6 tbsp/75g basmati rice (or 1 cup/190g cooked rice, if starting with leftovers)
ingredients.original: 6 tbsp/75g basmati rice (or 1 cup/190g cooked rice, if starting with leftovers)

ingredients.item: 2 medium very ripe tomatoes, finely chopped (1½ cups/270g)
ingredients.original: 2 medium very ripe tomatoes, finely chopped (1½ cups/270g)

ingredients.item: 2 small Persian cucumbers (or ½ medium English cucumber), finely chopped; no need to peel or seed (1¼ cups/180g)
ingredients.original: 2 small Persian cucumbers (or ½ medium English cucumber), finely chopped; no need to peel or seed (1¼ cups/180g)

ingredients.item: 1½ tsp sumac, plus ¼ tsp to serve
ingredients.original: 1½ tsp sumac, plus ¼ tsp to serve

method: Put the rice, oil, turmeric, and ⅛ tsp of salt into a small saucepan with a lid. Mix well, until the rice is well coated, then pour in the water. Bring to a boil over high heat, then decrease the heat to low. Cover the pan and cook for about 17 minutes, or until the rice is cooked. Spoon the rice onto a plate and set aside until completely cool.
To make the tabbouleh, place all the ingredients in a large mixing bowl. Add the cooked rice, mix well, then spoon onto a large serving plate. Sprinkle with the remaining ¼ tsp sumac and serve.
